preconditions: KOZO trap path V1 is complete
Odin and Rust toolchains are installed
assumptions: Execution proof is validated through context-aware static analysis at this phase
K_INVALID is the current generated error-equivalent status constant

steps.title: Implement the ordered Odin syscall arbiter path and the Rust consumer contract
Add execution proof validation and detailed verify artifact reporting

expected_outputs: The Odin dispatcher proves the nil guard, magic guard, ordered mutation, and ABI-backed return flow
The Rust service proves the initialization, status handling, and postcondition expectations of the syscall contract
The harness records a granular execution_proof result in latest_verify.json

invariants: All syscall success and failure paths use generated ABI constants
The Odin source orders guards before payload mutation
The Rust source proves success and failure handling explicitly
expected_behavior: Changing the Rust magic value to 0xBADFEED causes execution_proof to fail
Removing or reordering the Odin mutation path causes execution_proof to fail
actual_behavior: verify.sh emits execution_proof with a granular sub-result breakdown.
Changing the Rust magic value to 0xBADFEED causes execution_proof to fail.
The Odin arbiter enforces nil and magic guards before mutation and returns the ABI success constant after ordered field updates.

explanation_summary: Implemented the ordered Odin arbiter path with nil guard, magic guard, stable ingress/egress tracing, pointer-based mutation, and ABI-backed return values.
Updated the Rust consumer to initialize the normative magic payload, branch on abi::K_OK, assert the postconditions, and enter a heavy failure path on error.
Added a granular execution proof validator so verify artifacts record field-level proof results instead of a single opaque status.
